query: Rafah crossing operator management Egypt EU monitors reopening 2026 agreement

{"query":"Rafah crossing operator management Egypt EU monitors reopening 2026 agreement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1469,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.eeas.europa.eu/eu-border-assistance-mission-rafah-crossing-point/rafah-crossing-point-reopens-eu-border-mission-resumes-operations_en?s=410503","title":"Rafah Crossing Point reopens as EU border mission resumes operations | EEAS","content":"The Rafah Crossing Point reopened on 19 March for the first time since operations were paused on 28 February due to the escalating security situation in the region following US-Israeli strikes on Iran
The EU Border Assistance Mission at the Rafah Crossing Point (EUBAM Rafah) resumed operations on 19 March following an agreement between Israeli, Egyptian, and Palestinian authorities to reopen the Gaza–Egypt border.
This includes 869 entries into Gaza and 875 exits, with 310 of those crossings involving medical cases."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/2/gazas-rafah-crossing-reopens-for-limited-traffic","title":"Just five medical patients leave Gaza after Israel reopens Rafah crossing | Gaza News | Al Jazeera","content":"Israel has reopened Gaza’s Rafah border crossing with Egypt for limited traffic after almost two years of closure, but only five Palestinian patients were permitted to leave Gaza on the first day, medical sources told Al Jazeera.
A vital entry point for humanitarian supplies and exit for people awaiting medical evacuation, the crossing is being reopened as stipulated under the United States-brokered “ceasefire” to halt Israel’s genocidal war on Gaza, which came into effect in October."},{"url":"https://www.thearabweekly.com/gazas-rafah-crossing-set-reopen-under-israeli-eu-and-egyptian-coordination","title":"Gaza’s Rafah crossing set to reopen under Israeli, EU and Egyptian coordination | | AW","content":"Reopening the border crossing was a key requirement of the first phase of US President Donald Trump’s plan to end the Israel-Hamas war.
Before the war, the Rafah border crossing with Egypt was the only direct exit point for most Gazans to reach the outside world as well as a key entry point for aid into the territory.
COGAT, the Israeli military unit that oversees humanitarian coordination, said the crossing will reopen in both directions for Gaza residents on foot only and its operation will be coordinated with Egypt and the European Union."},{"url":"https://www.dw.com/en/gaza-rafah-border-crossing-partially-reopens/a-75762674","title":"Gaza: Rafah border crossing partially reopens","content":"The Rafah crossing between the Gaza Strip and Egypt was partially reopened to pedestrians in both directions on Monday, the first time the key border post has been operational in over a year.
"As of this time, and following the arrival of the EUBAM teams on behalf of the European Union, the Rafah crossing has now opened to the movement of residents, for both entry and exit," an Israeli official told the AFP news agency, referring to the EU Border Assistance Mission (EUBAM).
"The opening of the Rafah crossing marks a concrete and positive step in the peace plan," said the European Union's foreign policy chief, Kaja Kallas, adding: "The EU's civilian mission is on the ground to monitor crossing operations and support Palestinian border guards."
The reopening of the Rafah crossing is part of a peace plan brokered by the United States, Qatar and other regional powers which has been in place since October 2025 — although both Israel and the Palestinian militant group Hamas have repeatedly accused each other of breaking it."},{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/international/article/2026/02/02/gaza-s-rafah-crossing-reopened-under-restrictive-israeli-conditions_6750045_4.html","title":"Gaza's Rafah crossing reopened under restrictive Israeli conditions","content":"Sick and wounded Gazans began crossing into Egypt to seek medical treatment on Monday, February 2, after Israel permitted a limited reopening of the Palestinian territory's Rafah border post.
Israeli troops still control more than half of Gaza, while the rest remains under Hamas authority."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Rafah crossing operator management Egypt EU monitors reopening 2026 agreement","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rafah_Border_Crossing","title":"Rafah Border Crossing - Wikipedia","content":"The **Rafah Border Crossing** (Arabic: معبر رفح, romanized: *Ma`bar Rafaḥ*) or **Rafah Crossing Point** is the sole crossing point between Egypt and Palestine's Gaza Strip and Gaza's sole border point with a country other than Israel. The Rafah crossing was opened by Israel after the 1979 peace treaty and remained under Israeli control until 2005, when it was transferred to Egyptian, Palestinian Authority, and EU control, giving Palestinians partial control of an international border for the first time. Under a 2007 agreement between Egypt and Israel, Egypt controls the crossing but imports through the Rafah crossing require Israeli approval. Israel took control of the crossing on 7 May 2024, during the Gaza war, before withdrawing in January 2025 as part of a ceasefire agreement. On 2 February 2026, the crossing was reopened, but since the beginning of the Iran war, it was closed again. Before Israel's disengagement from Gaza on 11 September 2005, the main border crossing at Rafah between Egypt and Gaza was controlled by Israeli authorities.","score":0.7738498,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rafah","title":"Rafah","content":"+ 2.1 Late Bronze Age. # Rafah. Palestinian city in southern Gaza Strip. This article is about the Palestinian city in the Gaza Strip. For the part of the city in North Sinai, see Rafah, Egypt. | Location of Rafah in the Gaza Strip Location of Rafah in the Gaza Strip |. **Rafah** (Arabic: رفح *Rafaḥ* ) is a largely destroyed and depopulated city in the southern Gaza Strip, Palestine, that serves as the capital of the Rafah Governorate. Due to the Gaza war, about 1.4 million people from Gaza City and Khan Yunis, about 70% of Gaza's population, were displaced to Rafah, as of February 2024. By April 2025, most of the city had been destroyed by Israeli forces and fell under Israeli control. During the 1967 Six-Day War, Israeli forces re-occupied the Sinai Peninsula and Gaza Strip after capturing them from Egypt. Rafah is the site of the Rafah Border Crossing, the sole crossing point between Egypt and the Gaza Strip.","score":0.2778358,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.britannica.com/place/Rafah","title":"Rafah | Gaza, Map, Offensive, Crossing, & Population","content":"Rafah is a city on the border of the Gaza Strip and Egypt that has been divided for much of the 20th and 21st centuries, with its eastern part in Gaza and its western part in Egypt. In May 2024, the IDF ordered an evacuation of Rafah and took control of the Rafah border crossing. Historically, Rafah has been a border town between Egyptian and Syrian powers. Rafah is a city situated along the border of the Gaza Strip and Egypt. Historically, Rafah has been divided, with its eastern part in the Gaza Strip and its western part in Egypt. The Rafah border crossing, south of the city, was the Gaza Strip’s only border crossing not controlled by Israel between 2005 and 2024. Rafah is a city divided between the Gaza Strip and Egypt. Rafah has a history as a border town between Egypt and Syria.","score":0.27148506,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rafah,_Egypt","title":"Rafah, Egypt - Wikipedia","content":"# Rafah, Egypt. For the part of the city in Gaza Strip, Palestine, see Rafah. City in North Sinai, Egypt. | Rafah is located in Egypt Rafah  Rafah  Location in Egypt |. **Rafah** (Arabic: رفح, IPA: ) is a city in North Sinai and Egypt's eastern border with the Gaza Strip, Palestine. It is the capital of Rafah center in North Sinai Governorate, and is situated on the eastern Mediterranean coast of Egypt. Rafah is the site of the Rafah Border Crossing, the sole crossing point between Egypt and the Gaza Strip.*[clarification needed*] Following the 2013 military coup, the new Egyptian government announced in early 2015 that it would raze the entire city and build a new settlement for its residents, in order to expand a security buffer between Egypt and the Gaza Strip. In October 2014, the Egyptian government announced plans to relocate the population and completely demolish the city in order to enlarge the buffer zone between Egypt and Gaza.","score":0.26453808,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.doctorswithoutborders.org/latest/whats-happening-rafah","title":"What’s happening in Rafah? What You Should Know | Doctors Without Borders - USA","content":"* What is MSF doing in Gaza? * What is MSF’s mission? Learn how you can support MSF’s lifesaving care with a gift. + What We Do Discover how we bring lifesaving care to those who need it most. + Where We Work Explore our work in more than 70 countries. + Who We Are A global movement providing lifesaving care. For months, the southern city of Rafah functioned as a refuge for more than 1.5 million Palestinians, many of whom fled the conflict and bombardments in other parts of Gaza that have killed more than 45,000 people to date. Shortly after Israeli forces issued evacuation orders and escalated their military activities in Rafah, they seized control of the Rafah crossing and closed it until further notice, blocking people's access to lifesaving goods and supplies and making it near-impossible for humanitarian organizations to sustain lifesaving operations. Rafah is a city on Gaza's southern border with Egypt.","score":0.19456702,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.8,"request_id":"b122e6d6-3999-42e3-8818-d43aa2633380"}
